segments.text:
Pneumococcal Disease
CLS-N390 Vaccines: Science and Society

Gram positive cocci in chains -- Often hang out in pairs -- Lancet shape (peanut) Same genus as strep throat ( S. pyogenes ) and neonatal meningitis ( S. agalactiae ) 92 serotypes --10 most common cause about 62% of invasive disease worldwide
Streptococcus pneumoniae

Transmission
Found all over -- Normal bacteria in back of throat for some – carrier
Spread via direct contact with respiratory secretions – saliva and mucous
Many people have had it in nose or throat without being ill
Age and heath conditions increase risk for pneumococcal disease

At Risk
Children at risk | Adults as risk
Younger than 2 years old and those with: 
Sickle cell disease, no spleen, HIV infection, cancer, or another condition that weakens the immune system
Diabetes
Nephrotic syndrome
Chronic heart, lung, or kidney disease
Cochlear implants
Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) leaks (escape of the fluid that surrounds the brain and spinal cord) | Adults 65+

Adults with: 
Sickle cell disease, no spleen, HIV infection, cancer, or another condition that weakens the immune system
Diabetes
Nephrotic syndrome
Chronic heart, lung, kidney, or liver disease
Cochlear implants
CSF leaks
Alcoholism

Adults who smoke cigarettes or have chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), emphysema, and asthma

Symptoms
May cause many different types of infections
Middle ear infection
Sinus infection
Pneumoniae
Meningitis
Bloodstream infection
Sepsis

Sinus infection
Middle ear infection
Symptoms
Common symptoms include:
Headache
Stuffy or runny nose
Loss of sense of smell
Facial pain or pressure
Postnasal drip
Complications are rare, by may be:
Infection of tissue surrounding eye
Bone infection
Abscess (collection of pus)
Common symptoms of otitis media include:
Ear pain
A red, swollen ear drum
Fever
Sleepiness
Usually mild and more common than severe forms of disease
Children with repeated ear infections may need ear tubes

Pneumonia
Symptoms
Common symptoms include:
Fever and chills
Cough
Rapid breathing or difficulty breathing
Chest pain
Older adults may experience confusion or low alertness rather than common symptoms
Complications of pneumococcal pneumonia include:
Infection of space between membranes that surround lungs and chest cavity (empyema)
Inflammation of sac surrounding heart (pericarditis)
Blockage of airway allowing air into the lungs with collapse within the lungs and collection of pus in the lungs
Pneumococcal pneumonia kills about 1 in 20 who get it

Blood infection
Sepsis
Symptoms
Symptoms of pneumococcal bacteremia include:
Fever
Chills
Low alertness
About 1 in 30 children and 1 in 7 adults die of pneumococcal bacteremia
Survivors may have loss of limb(s)
Sepsis is an extreme response to a blood infection. Symptoms include:
Confusion or disorientation
Shortness of breath
High heart rate
Fever, shivering, or feeling very cold
Extreme pain or discomfort
Clammy or sweaty skin
Complications include kidney failure and damage to brain, lungs, or heart

Meningitis
Symptoms
Symptoms of pneumococcal meningitis include:
Stiff neck
Fever
Headache
Photophobia
Confusion
In babies, meningitis may cause poor eating and drinking, low alertness and vomiting
About 1 in 12 children and 1 in 7 adults die of pneumococcal meningitis
Survivors may have long-term problems – hearing loss or developmental delays

Pneumococcal Vaccine Timeline
1977
1983
2000
2010
14-valent polysaccharide vaccine licensed
23-valent polysaccharide vaccine licensed
7-valent polysaccharide conjugate vaccine licensed
13-valent polysaccharide conjugate vaccine licensed
2011
13-valent vaccine approved for 50+

Pneumococcal Vaccines
Pneumovax 23 (Merck)
23-valent polysaccharide
23 types of bacteria causing 60%-76% of invasive disease
Single vial or 5 dose vial
Phenol preservative
For 2 yrs +
60% - 70% effective
Less effective against pneumococcal pneumonia
7 valent polysaccharide conjugate
7 types of bacteria
Conjugated to nontoxic variant of diphtheria toxin (CRM197)
13-valent polysaccharide conjugate
13 types of bacteria
Conjugated to CRM197
Polysorbate 80 – surfactant
Aluminum phosphate – adjuvant
Succinate buffer
No thimerosal preservative
